fixtures.purpose: Compacted graph whose type (Person), relation (KNOWS), and tags (café, Active) are key-safe UTF-8 strings, not snake_case, plus a node whose type is exactly 64 bytes. A conformant reader must accept all of these (CONF-1/CONF-2: casing and word-separation are an SDK convention, not a format rule; the cap is 64 bytes).

fixtures.purpose: Major-2 regression lock for the tag-index key collision: two nodes share the id 'shared' across types (person, project) and both carry the tag 'topic'. The type-qualified tag key (t:{tag}:{type}:{id}) keeps them distinct, so the file materializes and reads clean; under the major-1 key shape it could not exist.

fixtures.purpose: PutNode and DeleteNode in a single commit. Node must be absent after open.
PutNode committed, then DeleteNode in a second commit. Node must be absent after reopen.
PutNode×2, PutEdge, and DeleteEdge in a single commit. Edge must be absent; nodes remain.
PutNode×2+PutEdge committed, then DeleteEdge in a second commit. Edge must be absent after reopen; nodes remain.
Store-level open tolerates a structurally valid unknown section while hydrating known Data/Bloom/WAL sections.

fixtures.purpose: Malformed committed WAL record that ordinary open must reject.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from an empty valid store container with a committed PUT_EDGE WAL record; the length field (bytes 9–12) is overwritten to 0x7FFFFFFF, triggering the buffer-size check in decodeWALRecord before the CRC check, yielding errInvalidWALRecord.

fixtures.purpose: Data primary/derived index mismatch that ordinary open must reject.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a valid small graph Data section, then a derived index key is changed/removed so the authoritative primary records no longer match regenerated derived keys.

fixtures.purpose: Wrong magic bytes in the container header must fail ordinary open.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a valid empty store container, then the first magic byte is flipped.

fixtures.purpose: Unsupported major version must fail closed even when the header checksum is internally consistent.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a v2 header using the deterministic container encoder.

fixtures.purpose: Bad header checksum must fail before section decoding.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a valid empty store container, then one header checksum byte is flipped.

fixtures.purpose: Bad section checksum must fail ordinary open.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a valid empty store container, then one trailing section checksum byte is flipped.

fixtures.purpose: Duplicate Data sections are invalid because v1 requires exactly one Data section.
fixtures.corruption: Generated as a structurally encoded container with two Data section table entries.

fixtures.purpose: Overlapping Data and Bloom section ranges must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with valid section payload bytes but overlapping section table offsets.

fixtures.purpose: Malformed Bloom payload must fail ordinary store open.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a one-byte Bloom payload that cannot satisfy v1 Bloom shape checks.

fixtures.purpose: Unknown WAL opcode must be rejected before replay.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a WAL record carrying opcode 0xff and a valid record checksum.

fixtures.purpose: Committed PUT_NODE WAL record with malformed payload must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with an invalid MessagePack payload for a committed PUT_NODE record.

fixtures.purpose: Committed PUT_NODE WAL record with invalid UTF-8 in a nested node meta string must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a syntactically valid MessagePack node payload containing invalid UTF-8 in nested meta.

fixtures.purpose: Committed DELETE_NODE WAL record with malformed payload must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with an invalid MessagePack payload for a committed DELETE_NODE record.

fixtures.purpose: Committed PUT_EDGE WAL record with malformed payload must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with an invalid MessagePack payload for a committed PUT_EDGE record.

fixtures.purpose: Committed DELETE_EDGE WAL record with malformed payload must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with an invalid MessagePack payload for a committed DELETE_EDGE record.

fixtures.purpose: Malformed committed WAL record checksum must fail ordinary open rather than being salvaged.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a committed WAL batch, then one byte in the first committed record is flipped.

fixtures.purpose: Committed WAL prefix with duplicate sequence numbers must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a committed WAL batch whose COMMIT record reuses the preceding mutation sequence number.

fixtures.purpose: Committed WAL prefix with decreasing sequence numbers must be rejected.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a committed WAL batch whose COMMIT record sequence number is lower than the preceding mutation sequence number.

fixtures.purpose: Primary Data node key with malformed node payload must fail hydration.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a primary node Data key whose value is invalid MessagePack, with Bloom regenerated for the damaged Data keys.

fixtures.purpose: Primary Data node key with invalid UTF-8 in a nested meta string must fail hydration.
fixtures.corruption: Generated with a syntactically valid MessagePack node payload containing invalid UTF-8 in nested meta, with Bloom regenerated for the Data keys.

fixtures.purpose: Node Data with a tag but missing the required tag-derived key must fail derived-key consistency checks.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from canonical node Data entries, then the required tag-derived index entry is omitted while Bloom matches the remaining keys.

fixtures.purpose: Primary Data node key whose type is 65 bytes — one over the 64-byte cap (CONF-2). A conformant reader must reject it on load.
fixtures.corruption: Generated from a valid single-node store whose type sits exactly at the 64-byte cap, then the type is extended by one byte in every key that embeds it (Bloom regenerated to match), so the 64-byte cap — not an identity or derived-index mismatch — is what fails.
